Some improvements have been made (e.g. making it easier to pick the closest location, and the checkout seems faster), but more difficulties have been added.   I’m really tired of closing extra windows trying to sell me things I’m not interested in, having to click “not today”, and having 1/3 of other screens occupied with something I don’t order with a big “Order Now” button next to it.   Please give me an “Order Never” button!  I’m still tired of there being a morning Egg McMuffin and an after 11:00 a.m Egg McMuffin which apparently is a totally different thing (Just try and reorder a morning McMuffin in the afternoon, and pray you never order at 10:59 and have to redo your order!).  And today, I requested “no butter” on the app, and the kitchen labels printed “CHEESE only” instead!  At least the crew person had the initiative to check with me while making up my McMuffin with cheese only and saved me a trip back.  (NOTE: this last one has been fixed, so they seem to listen to problem reports).  Also… being able to add a gift card to your account should be doable here, as it is on many other apps.

I have been using the McD app ever since it was released and I enjoy the ability to order anything from the menu and make customizations. However, there are several issues with app performance. First, it is too slow, especially loading it for the first time (and I use it pretty much daily), which makes it a challenge when I load the app while I’m in the drive thru waiting to order.
Second, once loaded, there always seems to me a promotion that I can’t quickly exit out of, even after tapping the “x” to close it. 
Third, getting a code takes way too long. 
Finally, this is tied to the first point but I always seem to have to log in with email verification. Again, not very efficient if you are waiting at the drive thru window for the app to go through all the motions listed above. 
I would suggest some improvements:
- have a code that can sit in your Apple wallet so that you can flip to scan. The terminals in the restaurant scanners have them already
- have the app use iOS’s passcode/faceID so you don’t have to log in every time and wait for a verification code. If someone has access to your phone and McD app, don’t you think the email account is accessible as well? Sorry to say but banking apps have far less steps to get access. 
- reduce the number of pop ups or find some other way to put your promos upon start up. Again, sometimes we just want our codes to give to the drive thru attendant

I will continue to use the app out of necessity but if someone is reading these, please consider these suggestions seriously.

40 years ago, my dad played a prank on me, having his friend pretend to “deliver” McDonald’s the same way you’d order a pizza or Chinese food back then: by phone. The prank worked and for the longest time I thought, “wow, McDonald’s delivers!” Of course I learned better as I got older. Today, my dad and his friend long since dead, I think they were faster than ordering in app in 2026: it’s slow, painfully slow; the deals are nice but the options at the kiosk (ha, a kiosk!) aren’t the same as in app (I like light sauce on a Big Mac, which I can only specify at the kiosk or cashier), and - this not an app issue, but I think considered globally ridiculous - I get that a lot of people start their day with a 4am breakfast, but a lot of people also end their day with a 4am dinner. If breakfast started at 5am, I’d eat breakfast. If dinner ended at 5am, I’d miss a Big Mac. At 4am, it makes no sense at all. I get the cost per customer issue, but it’s still kinda dumb. It makes me skip McDonald’s the next day, and I eat here regularly, maintain a decent shape and health, love the food, and can’t get what I like because some genius thought that everyone eats breakfast in Canada at 4am. We’ve evolved from eating dead seals.
